parameters.name: type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description: The noise function used to generate noise. The functions available are:

parameters.name: seed
parameters.label: Seed
parameters.description: Any number, integer or non-integer, which starts the random number generator. Each number gives completely different noise patterns, but with similar characteristics.

parameters.name: period
parameters.label: Period
parameters.description: The approximate separation between peaks of a noise cycle. It is expressed in Units. Increasing the period stretches the noise pattern out.

parameters.name: periodunit
parameters.label: Period Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, Seconds, or Fraction.

parameters.name: harmon
parameters.label: Harmonics
parameters.description: The number of higher frequency components to layer on top of the base frequency. The higher this number, the bumpier the noise will be (as long as roughness is not set to zero). 0 harmonics give the b

parameters.name: spread
parameters.label: Harmonic Spread
parameters.description: The factor by which the frequency of the harmonics are increased. It is normally 2. A spread of 3 and a base frequency of 0.1Hz will produce harmonics at 0.3Hz, 0.9Hz, 2.7Hz, etc. This parameter is on

parameters.name: rough
parameters.label: Roughness
parameters.description: Controls the effect of the higher frequency noise. When roughness is zero, all harmonics above the base frequency have no effect. At one, all harmonics are equal in amplitude to the base frequency. Wh

parameters.name: exp
parameters.label: Exponent
parameters.description: Pushes the noise values toward 0, or +1 and -1. (It raises the value to the power of the exponent.) Exponents greater than one will pull the channel toward zero, and powers less than one will pull pea

parameters.name: numint
parameters.label: Num of Integrals
parameters.description: Defines the number of times to integrate (see the Area p. 114) the Brownian noise. Higher values produce smoother curves with fewer features. Values beyond 4 produce somewhat identical curves. This pa

parameters.name: amp
parameters.label: Amplitude
parameters.description: Defines the noise value's amplitude (a scale on the values output).

parameters.name: reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: Only available if operator's Parameter is on. Toggling this parameter will reset the noise calculation and hold the value until the parameter is released again.

parameters.name: resetpulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: Only available if operator's Parameter is on. Pulsing this parameter will reset the noise calculation.

parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Changing the Transform Order will change where things go much the same way as going a block and turning east gets you to a different place than turning east and then going a block. In matrix math term

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: As with transform order (above), changing the order in which the rotations take place will alter the final position and orientation. A Rotation order of Rx Ry Rz would create the final rotation matrix

parameters.name: constraint
parameters.label: Constraint
parameters.description: Constraint and its parameters allows the noise curve to start and/or end at selected values. The mean value may also be enforced. This only works when is Off because time slicing has no pre-determined

parameters.name: constrstart
parameters.label: Starting Value
parameters.description: Value for the starting position.

parameters.name: constrend
parameters.label: Ending Value
parameters.description: Value for the ending position.

parameters.name: constrmean
parameters.label: Mean Value
parameters.description: Value for the mean value of the noise.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.label: Normalize
parameters.description: Ensures that all noise curves fall between -1 and 1. Applied before the Amplitude parameter. Only valid for Random and Harmonic Summation noise types, since Hermite and Sparse noise are always normali

parameters.name: channelname
parameters.label: Channel Names
parameters.description: You can creates many channels with simple patterns like " ", which generates 20 channels from chan1 to chan20. See the section, Common Parameters for a description of this and all Options. See Options

parameters.name: start
parameters.label: Start
parameters.description: Start of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).

parameters.name: startunit
parameters.label: Start Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: end
parameters.label: End
parameters.description: End of the interval, expressed in Units (seconds, frames or samples).

parameters.name: endunit
parameters.label: End Unit
parameters.description: Select the units to use for this parameter, Samples, Frames, or Seconds.

parameters.name: rate
parameters.label: Sample Rate
parameters.description: The sample rate of the channels, in samples per second. Default:

parameters.name: left
parameters.label: Extend Left
parameters.description: The left extend conditions (before/after range).

parameters.name: right
parameters.label: Extend Right
parameters.description: The right extend conditions (before/after range).

parameters.name: defval
parameters.label: Default Value
parameters.description: The value used for the Default Value extend condition.

parameters.name: timeslice
parameters.label: Time Slice
parameters.description: Turning this on forces the channels to be " ".  A is the time between the last cook frame and the current cook frame.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.label: Scope
parameters.description: To determine which channels get affected, some CHOPs use a string on the Common page.

parameters.name: srselect
parameters.label: Sample Rate Match
parameters.description: Handle cases where multiple input CHOPs' sample rates are different. When Resampling occurs, the curves are interpolated according to the Interpolation Method Option, or "Linear" if the Interpolate Op

parameters.name: exportmethod
parameters.label: Export Method
parameters.description: This will determine how to connect the channel to the parameter. Refer to the article for more information.

parameters.name: autoexportroot
parameters.label: Export Root
parameters.description: This path points to the root node where all of the paths that exporting by are relative to.

parameters.name: exporttable
parameters.label: Export Table
parameters.description: The used to hold the export information when using the Table Methods (See above).

parameters.name: sz
parameters.label: Sz
parameters.description: XYZ scale to shrink or enlarge the transform.

parameters.name: pz
parameters.label: Pz
parameters.description: XYZ pivot to apply the above operations around.

parameters.name: rz
parameters.label: Rz
parameters.description: XYZ rotation, in degrees.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.label: Offset
parameters.description: Help Not Available.

parameters.name: tz
parameters.label: Tz
parameters.description: XYZ translation values.

examples.label: Configure smooth random animation
examples.code:
n = op('noise1')
n.par.roughness = 0.3
n.par.period = 5
n.par.amplitude = 1
n.par.channels = 'tx ty tz'
examples.description: Generate 3 smooth noise channels for position animation.

examples.label: Set deterministic seed
examples.description: Fix the seed for reproducible output across sessions.

examples.label: Read current noise value
examples.description: Evaluate the current value of the tx channel.
